description: Browse Salesforce org metadata directly from VS Code.
orgBrowser.activitybar.title: Salesforce Org Browser
orgBrowser.view.name: Salesforce Org Browser
org_browser_walkthrough_description: Browse and retrieve your org's metadata types and components using the Org Browser.
org_browser_walkthrough_open_description:
The Org Browser shows all metadata **in your org** — not what's in your local project. Expand any type to see its components.

**○ Open circle** — exists in your org, not yet downloaded locally.

**✓ Check circle** — exists in your org and is present in your local project.

[Open Org Browser](command:workbench.view.extension.metadata)

*[Org Browser Documentation](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/sfvscode-extensions/guide/org-browser.html)*
org_browser_walkthrough_open_alt: Org Browser sidebar showing list of metadata types with ApexClass expanded
org_browser_walkthrough_custom_object_de:
Expand any **Custom Object** to see its fields with type details — field type, length, scale, precision, and relationship names are shown inline.

This lets you understand your data model directly in VS Code without opening Setup.
org_browser_walkthrough_custom_object_al: Broker__c custom object expanded showing fields with type details
org_browser_walkthrough_retrieve_type_ti: Retrieve All Components of a Type
org_browser_walkthrough_retrieve_type_de:
Hover over any **metadata type row** to reveal the retrieve icon. Click it to download every component of that type to your local project in one step.

Use the **refresh icon** on the same row to re-fetch the component list from your org.
org_browser_walkthrough_retrieve_type_al: ApexClass type row with Retrieve Metadata tooltip visible
org_browser_walkthrough_retrieve_single_:
Hover over any **individual component** to reveal its retrieve icon. Click it to download just that file.

The retrieved file opens automatically in the editor. A checkmark indicates the component now exists in your local project.
FileUtilities.cls open in editor with checkmark on component in Org Browser
